reviews.review_description: I'm 50 minutes in, at level 7 of 16. I love the look and the voice acting, but there are some dealbreakers: neck strain (due to the 3D nature of the maps), the map being very hard to memorize or perceptualize (a good minimap might help), repetitiveness, hard to control the mirrors/laser (especially when I need to look where my hands aren't), sluggish movement. All these make it a chore to play, instead of being challenged by the puzzle logic. I would give it a 5/5 if these quality-of-life issues are solved.

reviews.review_title: I don't review much, but when I do...
reviews.review_description:
I just bought the full version and it goes way beyond the demo. I'm only a few rooms past the demo and already my brain hurts. It's easy to get lost.
I love the Escher feel of up is everywhere at once.
Great game, great puzzles and I like the retro graphics.

And thank you for keeping the price low. I often pass on great games because of the cost.

(Now, where was that door again?)

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind feedback. We're more than happy you like our game! Keep on searching for the next door:)

reviews.review_description: Engaging story and immersive environments with challenging puzzles. Great puzzler that I have included in my top 10 app lab list: https://youtu.be/8ZG7KHOOan8
Lasers, mirrors and prisms, you can do some pretty cool things with them. I really have a lot of fun with this game. And I love the characters in the story. They give the game a unique touch.

First impressions review here: https://youtu.be/GxWHHOwxJnE

There's something very satisfying about moving those heavy mirrors around to create a chain of light to escape each room, and the chatter between yourself and F.R.I.D.A.Y is always entertaining!

reviews.review_title: Puzzle Game on the next level
reviews.review_description: Great and engaging story, especially the banter between Robin and Friday is quite amusing. The puzzles are hard enough for a casual playthrough and the perspective changes aren't as disorienting as I first expected. The visual style is also quite unique for VR and I love it.
